# tiki

> Terminal-first Markdown workspace for tasks, docs, and notes — stored in git, queried with ruki, extended with plugins and AI skills

tiki manages Markdown documents under `.doc/`. Each document is identified by a bare frontmatter `id`; identity lives in the frontmatter, not the filename, so files can move freely without breaking references. Documents carrying workflow fields (`status`, `priority`, …) show up on board and list views; documents with only `id` and `title` act as wiki pages. A bundled Markdown viewer renders images, Mermaid diagrams, and link navigation.

`workflow.yaml` defines statuses, fields, and views; kanban is one bundled workflow. Each plugin uses **ruki** — an SQL-like language for selecting, filtering, sorting, and updating documents. Triggers (ruki statements that fire on document changes) enforce workflow rules. AI skills let Claude Code, Gemini CLI, Codex, and Opencode manage documents through natural language.

## Docs

- [Quick Start](docs/quick-start.md): getting started — markdown viewer, file management, tiki board, keyboard shortcuts
- [Tiki Format](docs/tiki-format.md): .doc directory structure, tiki Markdown+YAML spec, field types
- [Configuration](docs/config.md): config.yaml, workflow.yaml, config directories, settings reference
- [Custom Statuses and Types](docs/customization/custom-status-type.md): type and status definition rules, validation, key normalization, inheritance
- [Customization](docs/customization/customization.md): workflow statuses, plugin definitions, lanes, actions, views
- [Installation](docs/install.md): macOS, Linux, Windows, manual install
- [Markdown Viewer](docs/markdown-viewer.md): pager commands, link navigation, image/diagram rendering
- [Quick Capture](docs/quick-capture.md): creating tikis from CLI without opening the TUI

## Ruki Language

- [Quick Start](docs/ruki/quick-start.md): mental model, CRUD operations, conditions, pipes, triggers
- [Examples](docs/ruki/examples.md): practical select/update/trigger statements with explanations
- [Syntax](docs/ruki/syntax.md): lexical structure, grammar, EBNF, operator tokens
- [Semantics](docs/ruki/semantics.md): statement behavior, trigger execution, expression evaluation
- [Triggers](docs/ruki/triggers.md): trigger configuration, patterns, execution pipeline, error handling
- [Types and Values](docs/ruki/types-and-values.md): value types, field catalog, type coercion
- [Operators and Built-ins](docs/ruki/operators-and-builtins.md): operator precedence, built-in functions

## AI Skills

- [AI Collaboration](docs/ai.md): overview of AI integration, supported tools, prompt conventions
- [Tiki Skill](ai/skills/tiki/SKILL.md): AI skill for creating, querying, updating, and deleting tikis

## Optional

- [Customization Examples](docs/ideas/plugins.md): plugin recipes — assign, tags, Claude integration, scoped views
- [Trigger Ideas](docs/ideas/triggers.md): workflow trigger patterns — WIP limits, auto-assignment, staleness
- [Themes](docs/themes.md): theme configuration and available palettes
- [Image Requirements](docs/image-requirements.md): terminal image protocol support, SVG rendering dependencies
- [Validation and Errors](docs/ruki/validation-and-errors.md): ruki validation layers, error types, diagnostics
- [Contributing](CONTRIBUTING.md): development setup, PR workflow, coding conventions
